description:
You are acting as a release-engineering assistant.  
    Your home directory is /home/user.  
    A directory already exists at  

        /home/user/releases  

    Inside that directory is the CSV file  

        /home/user/releases/release_log.csv  

    It contains exactly the following eight lines (including the header):

        ID,Version,Commit,Date,Environment
        1,2.3.0,5ad88f9,2023-04-17,staging
        2,2.3.0,5ad88f9,2023-04-18,production
        3,2.3.1,a71e3dd,2023-05-01,staging
        4,2.3.1,a71e3dd,2023-05-02,production
        5,2.2.5,cd891ee,2023-03-10,production
        6,2.4.0,ab123df,2023-06-11,staging
        7,2.4.0,ab123df,2023-06-12,production
        8,2.5.0-beta,bad1337,2023-07-01,staging

    Task  
    ----  
    1. Read the Version column (the 2ᵈ column) from release_log.csv, **excluding the header**.  
    2. Produce a plain-text file named  

           /home/user/releases/all_versions.txt  

       that satisfies **all** of the following:  
       • Each distinct version string appears exactly once.  
       • The lines are sorted in ascending lexicographical order (standard ASCII sort is fine).  
       • No blank lines, no surrounding spaces, no extra columns—just the version string itself.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline (the final “\n”).  

    The required final file must therefore contain precisely five lines:

        2.2.5
        2.3.0
        2.3.1
        2.4.0
        2.5.0-beta

    3. Accomplish the extraction and creation of all_versions.txt with **one** shell command of your choice (e.g., a pipeline of standard POSIX utilities).  

    Verification  
    ------------  
    The automated checker will read /home/user/releases/all_versions.txt and compare it byte-for-byte with the five-line list shown above. Any deviation—including missing/extra whitespace, differing order, repeated versions, or missing trailing newline—will fail the test.
